api/spec/packages/aip/lib/rules.purpose: Custom TypeSpec linter rules enforcing AIP naming conventions and structural constraints for the v3 API spec. Rules run during `make gen-api` (tsp compile) and block or warn on violations before any OpenAPI or SDK artifact is generated.

patterns.name: createRule factory for every rule
patterns.description: Every rule must be exported via `createRule` from `@typespec/compiler`, returning a visitor-callback object. Never define rules as raw functions or classes.
patterns.example: export const myRule = createRule({ name: 'my-rule', severity: 'warning', messages: { default: paramMessage`...` }, create: (context) => ({ model: (node) => { ... } }) })

patterns.name: Two-tier severity: error blocks generation, warning informs
patterns.description: Rules whose violations corrupt wire format or SDK method names (enum values snake_case, operationId kebab-case, @friendlyName presence) use `severity: 'error'`. Style/doc rules use `severity: 'warning'`.
patterns.example: casingErrorsRule (enum member values) → severity: 'error'. docDecoratorRule (missing @doc) → severity: 'warning'.

patterns.name: Casing contracts by node type
patterns.description: Model names: PascalCase. Non-path model properties: snake_case. Path parameters ($path decorator): camelCase. Enum names/member names: PascalCase. Enum member values: snake_case. @operationId values: kebab-case. All verified via utils.js helpers.
patterns.example: casing.js checks `property.decorators.find(d => d.decorator.name === '$path')` before switching between camelCase and snake_case checks.

patterns.name: @friendlyName required on named types (inverted for *Endpoints/*Operations interfaces)
patterns.description: All named models, enums, unions, and interfaces must have @friendlyName EXCEPT interfaces ending in `Endpoints` or `Operations`, which must NOT have it. Violation is severity: 'error'.

patterns.name: Casing utilities live exclusively in utils.js
patterns.description: isPascalCaseNoAcronyms, isCamelCaseNoAcronyms, isSnakeCase, isKebabCase are exported from utils.js with PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID). All rule files import from utils.js; never inline regexes in rule files.

patterns.name: field-prefix EXCLUDED_PREFIXES governs exemptions
patterns.description: When 2+ fields share the same underscore prefix (e.g. billing_id, billing_status), a warning fires urging grouping under a nested object. AIP-standard prefixes (is, enable, disable, allow, etc.) are exempt via EXCLUDED_PREFIXES array in field-prefix.js.
patterns.example: New prefixes that should be globally exempt must be added to EXCLUDED_PREFIXES in field-prefix.js; otherwise all existing uses of that prefix trigger warnings.

patterns.name: Composition over inheritance: spread or `model is` preferred
patterns.description: Using `extends` without `@discriminator` on the base model triggers a warning. composition-over-inheritance.js distinguishes template instances (different message) from plain model extension using isTemplateInstance().
patterns.example: model Foo { ...BaseFields; }  // OK. model Foo extends Base { }  // warning unless @discriminator on Base.

key_file_guides.role: Single source for all casing helpers (isPascalCaseNoAcronyms, isCamelCaseNoAcronyms, isSnakeCase, isKebabCase) plus doc-comment string manipulation helpers (extractMarkdownFromDocComment, wrapMarkdownAsDocComment, detectNewline, getIndentBefore). PascalCase exceptions list maintained here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new casing helper must go here; inlining regex in rule files breaks consistency. PascalCase exceptions (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) are hardcoded — add new acronyms here if the regex incorrectly flags them.

key_file_guides.role: Two rules: `casing` (warning) for structural name casing; `casing-aip-errors` (error) for enum member values (must be snake_case). The modelProperty visitor explicitly checks for `$path` decorator before choosing camelCase vs snake_case.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Path parameter properties use camelCase — the `$path` decorator check is critical. Forgetting it causes false positives on path params.

key_file_guides.role: Error-severity rule requiring @friendlyName on all named models, enums, unions, and interfaces. The interface logic is INVERTED: *Endpoints/*Operations must NOT have @friendlyName; all others must.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Getting the inversion wrong (flagging Endpoints interfaces for missing @friendlyName) is the most common mistake. Check `isEndpointsOrOperations` logic carefully.

key_file_guides.role: Two rules: `doc-decorator` (warning on missing @doc for models/enums/unions/properties) and `doc-format` (async warning that Prettier-formats doc comment bodies). Models ending in `Response` are exempt from property-level @doc checks. Properties named `_` or `contentType` are always exempt.
key_file_guides.watch_for: doc-format is async (Prettier 3.x); the rule declares `async: true` and uses an `exit()` hook to batch-process all collected DocNodes after all visitors run. Do not make async rules sync.

key_file_guides.role: Warns when 2+ fields share the same underscore prefix within a model. EXCLUDED_PREFIXES exempts AIP-standard boolean-flag prefixes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New prefixes that should be globally exempt (e.g. AIP-defined boolean prefixes) must be added to EXCLUDED_PREFIXES or they trigger warnings on all existing and future uses.

key_file_guides.role: Warns against `field: T | null` — use `field?: T` instead. Only fires on non-optional union properties that include a null variant.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only checks `!property.node?.optional` — optional properties with `| null` are not flagged. The rule is narrowly scoped.

key_file_guides.role: Warning when `extends` is used without `@discriminator` on the base. Uses isTemplateInstance() to give a different message for template-based extension vs plain model extension.
key_file_guides.watch_for: isTemplateInstance check distinguishes template bases from plain bases — the messageId differs ('instance' vs 'default'). Ensure both message variants remain in sync.

anti_patterns: Defining rules as raw functions instead of using createRule — TypeSpec rule registration requires createRule for proper diagnostic reporting and registration
Using `| null` on model properties instead of the optional `?` modifier — triggers no-nullable warning
Adding a field-prefix exemption inline in the rule logic instead of in EXCLUDED_PREFIXES — breaks the single-source pattern
Using `extends` for composition without @discriminator on the base model — triggers composition-over-inheritance warning
Omitting @friendlyName, @doc, or @summary on new named types/operations — these are errors or warnings that surface in `make gen-api` and block CI lint

decisions.decision: Error severity for wire-format and SDK-method-name constraints; warning severity for stylistic consistency
decisions.rationale: Enum member values must be snake_case (JSON wire format) and @operationId must be kebab-case (SDK method name generation) — violations corrupt generated artifacts. Doc/composition rules are warnings to allow incremental adoption.

decisions.decision: PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) codified in utils.js
decisions.rationale: AIP naming conventions acknowledge common industry acronyms; without the exceptions list, names like OAuthToken or APIKey would be incorrectly flagged as non-PascalCase.

decisions.decision: field-prefix EXCLUDED_PREFIXES exempts AIP-standard boolean-flag prefixes (is, enable, disable, allow, etc.)
decisions.rationale: AIP itself defines standard boolean field prefixes (is_active, enable_x, disable_y) that intentionally repeat; grouping them under a nested object would violate AIP guidance.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new custom lint rule that warns when an operation lacks a @tag decorator
